pages.full_text:
We drove east + ended up having a good supper at the Stag at Liskeard before camping at Trethen Meanor, Liskeard. *** Sad, [has] been playground.

[Hand-drawn sketch of Royal Albert Bridge / Brunel's bridge over the River Tamar at Saltash, with labels:]
TAMAR BRIDGE 1961
I.K. BRUNEL ENG. 1859
R. Tamar

[Bottom left margin:] Cornwall
[Right margin, vertical:] Devon
[Left margin, vertical, near bottom:] Saltash
Exciting to see the Tamar
[Right margin, vertical:] Brunel's bridge, lots not written.
pages.route_sketch_description: Detailed pen sketch of the Royal Albert Bridge (Brunel's bridge) over the River Tamar at Saltash, viewed from ground level showing the bridge's distinctive spans and piers. Labels include 'TAMAR BRIDGE 1961', 'I.K. BRUNEL ENG. 1859', and 'R. Tamar' at bottom right. 'Cornwall' marked at bottom left and 'Devon' in the right margin, indicating the two counties the bridge connects.
pages.notes: The word before 'playground' in the top text is partially legible — reads approximately '[has] been playground' or possibly '[past] playground'; unclear. 'Trethen Meanor' is the diarist's spelling; likely Trethane Manor near Liskeard. The sketch appears to be an original hand-drawn illustration rather than a traced image. Year cannot be confidently determined from this page alone; the sketch labels reference 1859 (bridge construction date) and 1961 (Tamar road bridge), but these are historical references not diary dates. Volume position suggests late 1990s–early 2000s.

pages.full_text:
[Left margin, vertical text, partially legible:] Saltash
Exciting to see the Tamar
Brunel's bridge, lots not written.

[Page contains six colour photographs, arranged in a 2×3 grid, with printed captions:]
[Top left photo caption:] Helford Vista
Trebah Valley
[Top right photo caption:] The Higgledy-Piggledy Tree
The Rain Forest
[Middle left photo caption:] [illegible/no caption visible]
[Middle right photo caption:] [illegible/no caption visible]
[Bottom left photo caption:] 100 Year Old Tree Fern
[Bottom right photo caption:] Tarzan's Camp
photo_captions: The Higgledy-Piggledy Tree / The Rain Forest
pages.notes: This page is primarily a glued-in postcard or photo insert showing six colour photographs of Trebah Garden (near the Helford River, Cornwall). The printed captions identify features: 'Helford Vista / Trebah Valley', 'The Higgledy-Piggledy Tree / The Rain Forest', '100 Year Old Tree Fern', and 'Tarzan's Camp'. The middle row photos (third and fourth) may also have captions but they are not clearly legible in the scan. The left margin text ('Saltash / Exciting to see the Tamar / Brunel's bridge, lots not written.') appears to overflow from the left page and is written vertically along the gutter margin — attributed to this page as it is physically on the right page's binding edge. Handwritten page number '40' visible in the top-right corner.
